rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WONG et al VS. KAREN FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 12, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT KAREN FUNG SANG LUM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. As the Court previously determined in its Order Denying Motion for Summary Judgment and Granting in Part and Denying in Part Alternate Motion for Summary Adjudication filed December 20, 2012 at page 2, l. 9-15, this action is not logically related to the Clancy action so this action is not barred by Cal. Civ. Pro. 426.30. The responses to requests for admission cited by Defendants in their moving papers in this motion are not sufficient new evidence to cause the Court to grant this second motion for summary judgment. The motion for summary adjudication of the fifth and eighth causes of action on statute of limitations grounds is granted. Defendant shifted the burden and Plaintiff failed to oppose or raise a triable issue of fact. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Cal. Civ. Pro. § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN TAFFLER et al VS. COLDWELL BANKER REAL ESTATE LLC A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 12, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTs REID RANKIN and VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA DBA COLDWELL BANKER's Motion For Leave To Amend cross-Complaint. The motion to amend is granted. Even if the Court assumes that the proposed cross-complaint is compulsory, amendment is proper. "A policy of liberal construction of section 426.50 to avoid forfeiture of causes of action is imposed on the trial court. A motion to file a cross complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank, (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99. In this case, there is no substantial evidence of bad faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL A. TRUFANT VS. POLICE CHIEF RE: OFFICERS RONALD MCGOLDRICK AND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss And Special Motion To Dismiss Pltf'S Complaint Per Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 12, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM and OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK's Motion To Dismiss And Special Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S Complaint Per Ccp 425.16. Granted. No opposition was filed. Defendants have satisfied their burden of making a prima facie showing that the alleged wrongful conduct by Officer McGoldrick arises from protected activity under CCP § 425.16. Written and oral communications made by police officers within an official proceeding of the law falls within the purview of the anti-SLAPP statute. See Schaffer v. City and County of San Francisco 168 Cal. App. 4th 992, 1003-04 (2008); Bradbury v. Superior Court, 49 Cal. App. 4th 1008, 1116 (1996). Plaintiff Trufant has failed to satisfy her burden to show a probability of prevailing in the action; no opposition was filed to the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
